title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to organization, Organization and managers, Manager’ roles and
skills, Behaviour at work, Introduction to organization behaviour, Major
behavioural science disciplines contributing to OB, Challenges and
opportunities managers have in applying OB concepts, OB models
Motivation models and levels of OB model.

title: Module 2
content:
Introduction to individual behaviour, Values, Attitudes, Job satisfaction,
Personality, Personality Traits, Perception and individual decision making,
Importance of Perception in OB, Learning, Motivation at work, Managing
emotions and stress, Meaning and Definition of Stress and job performance
relationship, Work Stressors - Approaches to stress management and Coping
with stress. Impression Management. Emotions and Moods in workplace.

title: Module 3
content:
Groups in organizations – Influence – Group dynamics – Interpersonal
Communication:
Importance,
Types,
Barriers
to
communication,
Communication as a tool for improving Interpersonal Effectiveness, Team
building - Interpersonal relations - Johari Window – Group decision making
techniques. Meaning of conflict and its types, Conflict Redressal process,
Leadership & management, Theories of leadership- Trait theory, Behavioural
Theory, Contingency Theory, Leadership & Followership, How to be an
Effective Leader, Introduction to Transactional Analysis. – Ego states, Types
of transactions, Life positions.

title: Module 4
content:
Foundations of organization structure, Organization design, Organizational
culture, Factors affecting organizational climate, Organization change,
Managing across cultures, Diversity at work. Introduction to Human
Resource Management: Policies and Practices - Selection, Orientation,
Training & Development, Performance Appraisal, Incentives, Importance of
Organisational Change, Planned Change & OB Techniques, International
OB: An Introduction to Individual & Interpersonal Behaviour in Global
Perspectives. Organizational effectiveness – Benchmarking - TQM and Six
Sigma.
